payload.text: Add forget password feature in login screen, comment delete option. In Profile page, let me go to post when clicked on post thumbnail. Dont add image by default when post in feed happens, instead if no image is attached on post, make the text only post have a gray bg square svg that looks like an image but not. Lets do 1 by 1

**Considering Feature Implementation**

I've been examining how to implement the "Forgot Password" feature and a "Delete Comment" option. Currently, I'm focusing on user flow and security implications. Navigating to specific posts via thumbnails on the Profile page is also being considered. Furthermore, I'm analyzing the changes needed for feed posting, particularly regarding default image behavior.


**Analyzing Default Image Handling**

Currently, I'm concentrating on the placeholder image issue. Instead of random visuals, an SVG gray square will be rendered when a post has no associated image. I am updating related components and incorporating Supabase integration to handle the new functionality.


**Adding Single Post View**

I'm currently focused on the `onClick` action for post thumbnails.  I've decided to create a dedicated `/post/:postId` route and implement a `PostDetail` component. This involves updating `App.tsx` and creating the necessary files. I'm also addressing the default image handling within `Feed.tsx` and `PostCard.tsx` to accommodate posts without images. The approach will include passing an empty string or a placeholder marker to efficiently handle image-less posts.


**Handling Empty Post Images**

I'm now implementing the gray square SVG for text-only posts, which will make them look consistent with media posts. I'm updating `createPost` in `supabaseClient.ts` to gracefully handle the absence of images. I'm also planning to add the ability to reset passwords and delete comments within the services.


**Refining Component Interactions**

I'm now integrating comment deletion with the UI, checking user ownership. The placeholder SVG for text-only posts is also taking shape. Profile grid items will now link to individual posts via routing, improving user navigation. Feed updates include removing the default random image and correctly handling empty images. I am planning the integration of password reset and comment deletion APIs.



**Enhancing Single Post Display**

I'm now focusing on `Detail.tsx`, the single-post wrapper. It includes a back button for navigation. For text-only posts, I've decided to handle empty `image_url` fields in `Feed.tsx` and pass them as an empty string. Within `PostCard.tsx`, I'll check for this empty string and render a gray square SVG placeholder, maintaining aspect ratio, for text-only posts, ensuring a consistent user experience. This design also accounts for the "gray background square" feedback.


**Incorporating New Features**

I am now adding the "Forgot Password" UI within `Login.tsx`, and implementing password reset and comment deletion functions in `supabaseClient.tsx`. I've completed the `/post/:postId` route and integrated the `PostDetail` component in `App.tsx`. I am also implementing the logic within `PostCard.tsx` to handle the gray SVG placeholder when a post lacks an image URL. I'm focusing on user flows and ensuring all components integrate seamlessly.


**Adding Grid Item Linking**

I'm now integrating post-specific links into the grid items on the Profile page to facilitate navigation. I am focusing on modifying `Feed.tsx` and updating the `supabaseClient.ts` service with `getPost(postId)`. This means making the `image_url` field optional during post creation and handling empty strings, and implementing the `PostDetail.tsx` component to handle post display.


**Updating Component Logic**

I'm now integrating the "Forgot Password" feature by adding an `isForgot` state to `Login.tsx`. This will allow for conditional rendering of an email input field and "Reset" button when the "Forgot Password?" link is clicked. The `Profile.tsx` component is being updated to use `<Link>` components, enabling direct navigation to individual posts.  I am ensuring an empty image URL is handled by `Feed.tsx`. I am planning the implementation of a consistent SVG gray square within a 1:1 container to represent posts without images.


**Adding Guest Mode Support**

I'm expanding `getPost` in `supabaseClient.tsx` to handle guest mode, returning a mocked post if the user is not authenticated. I'm also planning to integrate this with the existing `Feed.tsx` and `PostCard.tsx` components to ensure a seamless experience for all users. I have considered this carefully.


**Integrating Guest Mode Support**

I'm expanding `getPost` in `supabaseClient.tsx` to handle guest mode, returning a mocked post if the user is not authenticated, like deleting comments. Also, I'm integrating this with the existing `Feed.tsx` and `PostCard.tsx` components to ensure a seamless experience. In `resetPassword`, I simplified the logic for a `redirectTo` URL. I believe everything is in order.


**Integrating Icon and Link Changes**

I'm now integrating the `Trash` icon for comment deletion in `PostCard.tsx` and wrapping the image within a `Link` component in `Profile.tsx`. In `Feed.tsx`, I'm double-checking the logic for empty image URLs.  Additionally, `Login.tsx` is being updated to toggle between login, register, and forgot password views. I'm proceeding with XML SVG generation for the placeholder and the overall design considerations.


**Implementing Error Handling**

I'm now focusing on robust error handling throughout the application. Specifically, I'm integrating checks for network issues and server responses in the authentication flow. I'm also planning error messages for failed post loads and incorrect user inputs. Finally, I'm ensuring all the API calls, including password resets and comment deletions, include comprehensive error states.

entries.description: Add methods for getPost, deleteComment, and resetPassword; update createPost to handle optional images.
New component for single post detail view.
Add comment deletion and handle placeholder for text-only posts.
Don't attach random image if no file selected.
